Setting Leaderboard Properties

Clicking on an individual leaderboard in the project tree in the Document View window enables you to set the properties for that leaderboard. The various types of leaderboards support different properties. They are explained in the following sections.

  • Setting the Properties of Competition Leaderboard Templates
  • Setting the Properties of Standard Leaderboards

Setting the Properties of Competition Leaderboard Templates

Competition Leaderboard Templates support the following modifiable properties.

Entry Expiration
Specifies the number of days before entries in the leaderboard expire.
Only keep the top 100 entries
If this box is checked, the leaderboard displays only the top 100 entries. If it is not checked, the leaderboard keeps all entries.
This is a teams leaderboard.
Check this box if the leaderboard will be used for displaying team stats.
Uses ELO Rating System
If this box is checked, ELO ratings are assigned to players or teams. For more information about ELO ratings, see The ELO Rating System.
Initial Player Rating (Ri)
Specifies the starting rating for each new player in the competition.
Rating Scale Factor (K)
Setting a rating scale factor controls how profoundly a match's results affect the participants' ratings.
Exponential Base
The rating function is exponential. The exponential base does not have to be base 10, which is the default. You can set it to other numeric bases with this drop-down list.
Max Weight (C) Rating Range Array
Use this list to set the range of your rating system, divide the range into sections, and assign a weight or scaling factor to each section in the range. Up to five sections can be defined. If, for example, you decide to have a range of 0 to 1000, you might divide the range into five sections of 200 points. Each section has its own constant C that acts as a weight or scaling factor. In this case, the first C value in the list would be applied to scores between 1 and 200. The next C value could be for scores between 201 and 400, and so on. The last range value would be the maximum of the range—in this example, 1000. The C value for that entry would be applied to all scores over 1000, which would be considered expert level.
Web-Enabled Leaderboard
If the current leaderboard is visible on the Web, the properties in this group are available. Set the leaderboard attributes you want displayed on the Web by specifying a list of attribute index numbers separated by commas. You can also specify the attributes containing PUIDs as a comma-separated list of index numbers. Finally, you need to set the locale-specific names of the leaderboard for every locale you want displayed.

Note    The terms "player unique ID (PUID)" and Gamertag are used interchangeably in this documentation.

Setting the Properties of Standard Leaderboards

Standard Leaderboards enable the modification of the following properties.

Reset Type
Indicates how often the leaderboards should be reset to zero. This is done on the following schedule.
Reset Type Schedule
Never N/A
Weekly Sundays
Monthly Jan 1, Feb 1, Mar 1, Apr 1, May 1, Jun 1, Jul 1, Aug 1, Sep 1, Oct 1, Nov 1, Dec 1
Bimonthly (every other month) Jan 1, Mar 1, May 1, Jul 1, Sep 1, Nov 1
Quarterly Jan 1, Apr 1, Jul 1, Oct 1
Semiannually (twice a year) Jan 1, Jul 1
Annually Jan 1

Note    The reset is performed at a random time between midnight and 0300, local time.

Maximum # of Attachments
Specifies the maximum number of attachments any given leaderboard may have.
Max Attachments Size

Contains the maximum size, in bytes, of any single attachment.

Note    The maximum size of an attachment for any one leaderboard must be less than 2 MB. The total size of all attachments must be less than 1 GB.

Only keep the top 100 entries
As with Competition Leaderboard Templates, Standard Leaderboards enable you to choose whether a leaderboard should keep only the top 100 entries. For more information, see Setting the Properties of Competition Leaderboard Templates above.
Arbitrate this leaderboard
If this box is checked, the input information is arbitrated before it is entered into the leaderboard. This helps prevent various types of cheating.
This is a teams leaderboard.
Check this box if the leaderboard will be used for displaying team stats.
Uses ELO Rating System
Like Competition Leaderboard Templates, Standard Leaderboards support ELO ratings. For more information about ELO ratings, see The ELO Rating System.
Web-Enabled Leaderboard
Like Competition Leaderboard Templates, Standard Leaderboards enable you to make stats visible on the Web. For more information, see Setting the Properties of Competition Leaderboard Templates above.
